surveys.respond.thankYou: Thank you for completing the survey for {{name}}.
surveys.respond.confirmCorrect: I confirm this value is correct
surveys.respond.proposeChange: I want to propose a change

metamodel.title: Metamodel Configuration
metamodel.regulations.description: Manage the compliance frameworks that drive the TurboLens compliance scanner and manual finding entry. Built-in regulations can be disabled but not deleted; you can add your own (e.g. internal control frameworks, sector-specific regulations).
metamodel.regulations.disableTip: Disable — keeps existing findings but removes the regulation from scans and dropdowns.
metamodel.regulations.builtInProtected: Built-in regulations cannot be deleted. Disable them with the toggle instead.
metamodel.regulations.empty: No regulations yet. Add one to enable manual or AI-driven compliance assessment.
metamodel.regulations.deleteConfirm: Delete the regulation "{{label}}"? Existing findings linked to it will remain visible in the compliance register under a muted tab.
metamodel.regulations.keyHelp: Lowercase identifier used internally and on stored findings. Cannot be changed later.
metamodel.regulations.descriptionHelp: Plain-language description of what to assess for this regulation. The AI scanner uses this text to guide its analysis. Leave empty to fall back to a generic assessment template.
metamodel.regulations.translationsHelp: Display label per locale. Leave empty to fall back to the default label.
turbolens_security_no_regulations_enable: No regulations are currently enabled. Add or re-enable a regulation under Admin → Metamodel → Regulations to run a compliance scan.
turbolens_security_ai_not_configured_reg: AI is not configured, so on-demand scans are unavailable. You can still browse the compliance register and add findings manually.
metamodel.deleteRelHasInstances: This relation type has {{count}} relation instance(s) linking cards together. Deleting it will permanently remove all of them.
metamodel.deleteRelBuiltInHidden: This built-in relation type will be hidden and can be restored later.
metamodel.deleteRelNoInstances: This relation type has no instances and will be permanently deleted.
metamodel.typeDrawer.label: Label
metamodel.typeDrawer.noRelations: No relations connected to this type.
metamodel.typeDrawer.visibleTooltip: Show this relation on the card detail page
metamodel.typeDrawer.mandatoryTooltip: Require this relation to be filled in
metamodel.typeDrawer.subtypeTemplateHelp: Toggle which fields are visible for cards of this subtype. Hidden fields will not appear in the card detail or creation form, and will be excluded from data quality scoring.
metamodel.typeDrawer.noFieldsDefined: No fields defined for this type yet.
metamodel.typeDrawer.deleteFieldConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the field "{{label}}" ({{key}})?
metamodel.typeDrawer.checkingFieldUsage: Checking how many cards use this field...
metamodel.typeDrawer.fieldUsedByCards: {{count}} card(s) have data for this field. Deleting it will permanently remove that data from all of them.
metamodel.typeDrawer.fieldSafeToDelete: No cards have data for this field. It can be safely deleted.
metamodel.typeDrawer.deleteSectionConfir: Are you sure you want to delete the section "{{name}}"?
metamodel.typeDrawer.deleteSectionWithFi: Are you sure you want to delete the section "{{name}}" and its {{count}} field(s)?
metamodel.typeDrawer.checkingSectionUsag: Checking how many cards use fields in this section...
metamodel.typeDrawer.sectionNoFields: This section has no fields. It can be safely deleted.
metamodel.typeDrawer.sectionUsedByCards: {{count}} card(s) have data for fields in this section. Deleting it will permanently remove that data from all of them.
metamodel.typeDrawer.sectionSafeToDelete: No cards have data for fields in this section. It can be safely deleted.
metamodel.fieldEditor.calculatedInfo: This field is managed by a calculation. The field type is locked to prevent breaking the formula. Labels and colors can be changed freely.
metamodel.fieldEditor.keyLockedReason: Field key cannot be changed after creation
metamodel.fieldEditor.optionUsedByCards: "{{label}}" is used by {{count}} card(s). Their value will be cleared on save.
metamodel.fieldEditor.optionSafeToRemove: No cards use "{{label}}". Safe to remove.
metamodel.stakeholderPanel.title: Stakeholder Roles
metamodel.stakeholderPanel.noActiveRoles: No active stakeholder roles. Create one below.
metamodel.graph.noTypes: No visible types to display. Create some card types first.
tags.title: Tag Management
tags.deleteGroupConfirm: Delete tag group «{{name}}»? All tags in this group will be removed from every card.
tags.deleteTagConfirm: Delete tag «{{name}}»? It will be removed from every card it is assigned to.
tags.restrictToTypesHelper: Leave empty to apply to all card types
roles.title: Role Management
roles.selectPrompt: Select a role to view and edit its permissions
roles.archivedBanner: This role is archived. Users assigned to it retain their current permissions but the role cannot be assigned to new users.
roles.keyHelperText: The role key cannot be changed after creation.
roles.defaultRoleHint: New users will be assigned this role automatically.
roles.adminPermissionsInfo: The admin role has full access to all features. Permissions cannot be modified.
roles.createKeyHelperText: Unique identifier (lowercase, underscores only). Cannot be changed later.
roles.archiveConfirm: Are you sure you want to archive the role {{label}}?
roles.archiveUserWarning: This role is currently assigned to {{count}} user(s). Affected users will retain their current permissions but the role will no longer be available for new assignments.
roles.archiveRestoreHint: You can restore an archived role at any time.
users.title: User Management
users.columns.name: Name
users.archivedRoleWarning: This user has an archived role. Consider reassigning.
users.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
users.invite.title: Invite User
users.invite.ssoHint: SSO is enabled. If no password is defined, the user will need to sign in via their SSO provider.
users.invite.emailHint: A password is required for local accounts. The user can sign in with the address and password you set.
users.invite.passwordRequiredLocal: A password is required because SSO is not enabled.
users.invite.passwordSsoHelperText: If set, the user can also sign in with this password instead of SSO.
users.invite.passwordHelperText: The user signs in with this password.
users.invite.requiredFields: Email and display name are required.
users.edit.title: Edit User
users.edit.ssoPasswordHint: This user authenticates via SSO. Password cannot be changed.
users.edit.archivedRoleWarning: This user has an archived role. Consider assigning a new active role.
users.edit.requiredFields: Email and display name are required.
settings.title: Settings
settings.logo.title: Logo
settings.logo.description: Upload a custom logo to replace the default Turbo EA branding in the navigation bar. Recommended: PNG or SVG with visible colors (not white-on-transparent). Max 2 MB.
settings.favicon.title: Favicon
settings.favicon.description: Upload a custom favicon for the browser tab icon and Apple touch icon. This can be different from the main logo. Recommended: square PNG, at least 64x64 px. Max 2 MB.
settings.appTitle.title: Application Title
settings.appTitle.description: The name shown in the browser tab, image alt text, and outgoing emails. Leave empty to use the default brand name.
settings.currency.title: Currency
settings.currency.description: Choose the currency used to display all cost values across reports, dashboards, and card details.
settings.currency.label: Display Currency
settings.dateFormat.title: Date Format
settings.dateFormat.description: Choose how dates are displayed throughout the application.
settings.dateFormat.label: Display Format
settings.bpm.title: BPM Module
settings.bpm.description: Enable or disable the Business Process Management module. When disabled, the BPM navigation item, BusinessProcess cards, their relationships, and BPM reports will be hidden from all users.
settings.bpm.visible: BPM features are visible to users
settings.ppm.title: PPM Module
settings.ppm.description: Enable or disable the Project Portfolio Management module. When disabled, the PPM navigation item, dashboard, Gantt chart, and status reports will be hidden from all users.
settings.ppm.visible: PPM features are visible to users
settings.grc.title: GRC Module
settings.grc.description: Enable or disable the Governance, Risk and Compliance module. When disabled, the GRC navigation item, the Risk Register, the Compliance overview and the Governance workspace will be hidden from all users.
settings.grc.visible: GRC features are visible to users
settings.fiscal.title: Fiscal Year
settings.fiscal.description: Set the starting month of your fiscal year. This affects budget planning and cost reporting in the PPM module.
settings.ai.title: AI Capabilities
settings.ai.description: Enable AI-powered description suggestions when creating or editing cards. Choose between a self-hosted Ollama instance or a commercial LLM provider.
settings.ai.providerOllamaDesc: Run models locally with Ollama. DuckDuckGo web search is used to provide context.
settings.ai.providerOpenaiDesc: Works with OpenAI, Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI, OpenRouter, LM Studio, and vLLM. DuckDuckGo search is used for context.
settings.ai.providerAnthropicDesc: Direct connection to the Anthropic Claude API. DuckDuckGo search is used for context.
settings.ai.providerUrlHelper: URL of your Ollama instance (e.g. http://ollama:11434 in Docker, http://localhost:11434 locally)
settings.ai.providerUrlHelperOpenai: API base URL. Use https://api.openai.com for OpenAI, or your provider's URL.
settings.ai.apiKeyHelper: Your provider API key. Stored encrypted — never visible after saving.
settings.ai.apiKeySet: API key is set. Enter a new value to replace it.
settings.ai.modelHelper: Recommended: gemma3:4b for description generation. Other options: llama3.1:8b, mistral, qwen2.5
settings.ai.searchInfo: DuckDuckGo is used automatically to search the web for context before generating descriptions.
settings.ai.enabledTypesHelper: Select which card types show the AI suggest button. Leave all unchecked to enable for all types.
settings.ai.testNoModel: Connected, but model not found. Available: {{models}}
settings.ai.modelPickerHelper: Select a model from your provider. Use «Test Connection» to refresh the list.
settings.ai.testNoModels: Connected to Ollama, but no models are installed. Run «ollama pull gemma3:4b» to install one.
settings.ai.providerDescription: Configure the AI provider used by all AI features. This provider is shared across description suggestions, portfolio insights, and other AI capabilities.
settings.ai.portfolioInsightsDescription: Generate AI-driven insights on demand in the portfolio report. Analyses the current view — including active grouping and filters — to surface strategic recommendations.
settings.registration.title: Self-Registration
settings.registration.description: Allow new users to create accounts themselves from the login page. When disabled, users can only be added by an administrator (or via SSO if configured). The first user can always register to bootstrap the admin account.
settings.sso.title: Single Sign-On
settings.sso.description: Enable Single Sign-On with an external identity provider. When enabled, users can sign in using their corporate account. New SSO users are automatically assigned the Viewer role unless pre-invited with a specific role. Manual registration is disabled when SSO is active.
settings.sso.providerHelper: Select the SSO identity provider your organization uses.
settings.sso.clientIdHelper.google: From Google Cloud Console > APIs & Services > Credentials
settings.sso.clientIdHelper.oidc: From your OIDC provider application settings
settings.sso.clientSecretHelper.microsof: From your Azure App Registration > Certificates & secrets
settings.sso.clientSecretHelper.google: From Google Cloud Console > APIs & Services > Credentials
settings.sso.clientSecretHelper.oidc: From your OIDC provider application settings
settings.sso.tenantIdHelper: Use "organizations" for multi-tenant (any Azure AD), or a specific tenant ID to restrict access.
settings.sso.googleDomainHelper: Restrict sign-in to a specific Google Workspace domain (e.g. yourcompany.com). Leave empty to allow any Google account.
settings.sso.issuerUrlHelper: The OIDC issuer URL. A .well-known/openid-configuration endpoint must be available at this URL.
settings.sso.redirectUriHint: Configure this in your identity provider's application settings:
settings.sso.googleSetupHint: In Google Cloud Console, create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID under APIs & Services > Credentials. Add the redirect URI above under Authorized redirect URIs.
settings.sso.oktaSetupHint: In Okta Admin, create a Web Application integration with OpenID Connect. Add the redirect URI above under Sign-in redirect URIs.
settings.sso.oidcSetupHint: Configure an OpenID Connect application in your identity provider. Ensure the issuer URL exposes a .well-known/openid-configuration endpoint with authorization, token, and JWKS URIs.
settings.sso.manualEndpointsLabel: Manual endpoints (optional) — provide these if the backend cannot reach the discovery document (e.g. Docker networking or self-signed certificates). All three must be set to skip auto-discovery.
settings.sso.authorizationEndpointHelper: The URL where users are redirected to authenticate (e.g. /protocol/openid-connect/auth).
settings.sso.tokenEndpointHelper: The URL used to exchange the authorization code for tokens (e.g. /protocol/openid-connect/token).
settings.sso.jwksUriHelper: The URL serving the JSON Web Key Set for verifying token signatures (e.g. /protocol/openid-connect/certs).
settings.mcp.title: MCP Integration
settings.mcp.description: Allow AI tools (Claude, Copilot, Cursor, etc.) to query Turbo EA data on behalf of authenticated users. Each user authenticates via SSO and sees only the data their permissions allow.
settings.mcp.requiresSso: SSO must be enabled before activating MCP integration. AI tools authenticate users through your corporate SSO provider.
settings.mcp.setupStep1: Add this redirect URI to your SSO app registration:
settings.mcp.setupStep2: Share the MCP Server URL (below) with your team. Users add it to their AI tool to connect.
settings.smtp.title: SMTP Configuration
settings.smtp.description: Configure SMTP settings to enable email notifications. If left empty, only in-app notifications will be delivered.
settings.smtp.appBaseUrlHelper: Used in email notification links. Leave empty for localhost.
settings.locales.title: Enabled Languages
settings.locales.description: Choose which languages are available for end users. Users can only switch between enabled languages, and the translation dialog will only show these locales.
calculations.title: Calculated Fields
calculations.noCalculationsHint: Create a calculation to auto-populate card fields based on formulas.
calculations.columns.name: Name
calculations.formulaPlaceholder: Start typing — suggestions appear for fields, functions, and relations
calculations.insertHint: Tab or Enter to insert · Esc to dismiss
calculations.requiredFields: Name, target type, target field, and formula are required.
calculations.testDescription: Search for a {{type}} to test this formula against. The result will not be saved.
calculations.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
eol.description: Automatically find End-of-Life data for your IT Components and Applications by fuzzy-matching their names against endoflife.date. Select a product match, choose the version, and bulk-link them.
eol.linkSuccess: Successfully linked {{count}} card(s) to EOL data.
eol.clickToSelect: Click a match to select version:
eol.matchScore: Match score: {{score}}% — Click to select version
eol.emptyState: Click "Search EOL Data" to scan your {{type}} against endoflife.date
eol.productHint: Select the version/cycle that matches your deployment.
surveyBuilder.basics.title: Survey Details
surveyBuilder.basics.name: Survey Name
surveyBuilder.basics.descriptionHelper: Optional internal description (not shown to respondents)
surveyBuilder.basics.messageHelper: This message will be shown to targeted users when they open the survey
surveyBuilder.target.title: Target Cards
surveyBuilder.target.filterSpecificHint: Send the survey only to these specific cards. Leave empty to target all cards of the selected type (further narrowed by the filters below).
surveyBuilder.target.filterRelatedHint: Only target cards that have a relation to one of these items (e.g., all Applications related to the Sales organization)
surveyBuilder.target.filterAttributesHin: Only target cards where specific attributes match a condition (e.g., cost greater than 10000, TIME rating is missing)
surveyBuilder.target.stakeholderRolesHin: Which roles should receive the survey for each matched card?
surveyBuilder.fields.title: Select Fields
surveyBuilder.fields.description: Choose which fields respondents should maintain or confirm for each {{type}}.
surveyBuilder.fields.noTypeSelected: Please select a card type first.
surveyBuilder.fields.noFields: This type has no configurable fields.
surveyBuilder.preview.title: Preview & Send
surveyBuilder.preview.noMatches: No cards matched your filters, or no stakeholders were found with the selected roles. Go back and adjust your targeting criteria.
surveyBuilder.validation.typeRequired: Please select a target card type
surveyBuilder.validation.rolesRequired: Please select at least one stakeholder role
surveyBuilder.validation.fieldsRequired: Please select at least one field
surveysAdmin.title: Surveys
surveysAdmin.emptyAll: No surveys yet. Create one to start collecting data from your team.
surveyResults.detail.title: Response: {{card}} — {{user}}
cardLayout.title: Card Layout
cardLayout.builtinFields.name: Name
webPortals.description: Web portals are public-facing pages that display cards to end users without requiring authentication. Configure which card type to display, apply filters, and customize the card layout.
webPortals.noPortalsHint: Create your first portal to share card data with end users.
webPortals.cardTypeHelper: Which type of cards to display in this portal
webPortals.filterSubtypesHelper: Only show cards of these subtypes
webPortals.filterTagsHelper: Only cards carrying at least one of these tags will be shown in the portal
webPortals.displayConfigHint: Choose which properties appear on the summary card and the expanded detail view. Visible select fields and relation types also become filter dropdowns on the portal automatically.
webPortals.publishedHint: Make this portal publicly accessible without authentication
webPortals.showLogoHint: Display the application logo in the portal header
webPortals.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional description displayed at the top of the portal
webPortals.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this portal? This action cannot be undone.
servicenow.connections.deleteConfirm: Delete this connection and all its mappings?
servicenow.mappings.noConnectionsHint: Create a ServiceNow connection first before configuring mappings.
servicenow.mappings.dialog.syncDirection: Controls which Pull/Push operations are available on the Sync Dashboard
servicenow.mappings.dialog.skipStagingWa: Changes will be applied immediately without review. Staged records will not be created.
servicenow.mappings.dialog.selectCardTyp: Select a Card Type above to get field suggestions.
servicenow.mappings.dialog.fieldDirectio: Per-field source of truth: which system's value wins during sync
servicenow.sync.noActiveMappings: No active mappings. Configure mappings first.
servicenow.sync.noStagedRecords: No staged records for this run.
metamodel.translationDialog.title: Manage Translations — {{label}}
metamodel.principles.description: Define the architecture principles that govern your IT landscape. Active principles are used by AI Portfolio Insights to evaluate your application portfolio.
metamodel.principles.empty: No EA principles defined yet. Add principles to guide your architecture decisions.
metamodel.principles.showInDelivery: Show EA Principles tab on the EA Delivery page
metamodel.principles.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the principle «{{title}}»?
metamodel.principles.titlePlaceholder: e.g. Reuse before Buy before Build
metamodel.principles.implicationsPlaceho: What are the practical consequences of following this principle?
principlesCatalogue.subtitle: Curated reference set of industry-standard EA principles. Pick the ones that fit your organisation and import them into your principles register.
turbolens_description: AI-powered vendor analysis, duplicate detection, and architecture recommendations powered by TurboLens.
turbolens_ai_required: TurboLens requires a commercial AI provider (Anthropic or OpenAI) with a valid API key. Please configure one in the AI tab first.
ai_third_party_warning_body: AI features may send card metadata, descriptions, and other portfolio data to the configured third-party provider for processing. Confirm this is aligned with your organisation's IT, security, and data-protection policies before enabling.
turbolens_no_vendor_data: No vendor analysis data. Run an analysis first.
turbolens_no_duplicate_data: No duplicate data. Run detection first.
turbolens_architect_description: Describe your business requirement and the AI will generate architecture recommendations based on your existing technology landscape.
turbolens_architect_disclaimer_body: This assessment leverages AI to generate recommendations, solution options, and a target architecture. It should be performed by a qualified IT professional (Enterprise Architect, Solution Architect, IT Leader) in collaboration with business stakeholders. The generated output requires professional judgment and may contain inaccuracies. Use the results as a starting point for further discussion and refinement.
turbolens_architect_phase1_intro: Please answer these clarifying questions about your requirement:
turbolens_architect_phase2_intro: Based on your answers, please provide more technical details:
turbolens_arch_diagram_error: Diagram render error — showing raw Mermaid code instead
turbolens_arch_no_gaps: All requirements covered by existing landscape. No capability gaps identified.
turbolens_settings_description: Manage connections to TurboLens instances for AI-powered analysis.
turbolens_no_connection_description: Set up an TurboLens connection in Admin Settings to start using vendor analysis, duplicate detection, and architecture AI.
turbolens_dashboard_no_data: No data available yet. Run an analysis to get started.
turbolens_no_vendors_hint: Run a vendor analysis to categorize your technology vendors.
turbolens_no_hierarchy_hint: Run vendor resolution to build the canonical vendor hierarchy.
turbolens_no_duplicates_hint: Run duplicate detection to find functional overlaps in your portfolio.
turbolens_no_modernizations_hint: Run a modernization assessment to identify upgrade opportunities.
turbolens_duplicates_description: Detect functional duplicate cards and identify modernization opportunities across your landscape.
turbolens_architect_options_intro: Based on your requirements, here are the recommended solution approaches:
turbolens_architect_select_objectives: Which business objectives does this solution support?
turbolens_architect_objectives_hint: Select one or more existing Business Objectives from your landscape. The AI will analyze their dependencies to map capabilities.
turbolens_architect_analyzing_capabiliti: AI is analyzing capabilities and dependencies...
turbolens_architect_enable_card: Enable this card and its relations
turbolens_architect_disable_card: Disable this card and its relations
turbolens_architect_dependency_diagram_h: Dashed borders indicate proposed new components. Solid borders are existing landscape items.
turbolens_architect_gaps_intro: Select the product you want to use for each capability gap:
turbolens_architect_recs_selected: {{count}} recommendation(s) selected — these will be used in the capability mapping.
turbolens_architect_products_selected: {{count}} product(s) selected — click below to analyse what dependencies are needed.
turbolens_architect_deps_intro: These are the integration dependencies required for your selected products to work in your environment:
turbolens_architect_no_deps: No additional dependencies needed — the selected products can integrate with your existing landscape.
turbolens_architect_analyzing_gaps: AI is analyzing gaps for the selected approach...
turbolens_architect_select_capabilities: Which business capabilities are you improving or introducing?
turbolens_architect_capabilities_hint: Select existing Business Capabilities from your landscape, or type new ones that don't exist yet.
turbolens_commit_objectives_hint: These objectives will be linked to the new initiative.
turbolens_no_assessments: No assessments yet. Complete an architecture assessment to see it here.
turbolens_security_description: On-demand compliance gap analysis against EU AI Act, GDPR, NIS2, DORA, SOC 2 and ISO 27001.
turbolens_security_never_scanned: No scan has run yet. Trigger one to populate findings.
turbolens_security_compliance_decision_h: Accepted as-is with a documented rationale.
Promoted to a Risk in the GRC register.
turbolens_security_compliance_accept_hel: Use Accept when you have reviewed the gap and explicitly decided not to remediate. The rationale is recorded against this finding and is preserved across re-scans.
turbolens_security_compliance_ai_confirm: Confirmed as AI — "Has AI Features" is set to Yes on this card.
turbolens_security_compliance_ai_rejecte: Reviewed and confirmed NOT AI — "Has AI Features" is set to No on this card.
turbolens_security_compliance_filter_fro: Filtered to {{status}} from the heatmap. Clear to see all findings.
turbolens_security_compliance_ai_detecte: The scanner flagged this card as embedding or providing AI capabilities. Open the finding to confirm or reject the verdict — your answer updates the card's "Has AI Features" attribute.
turbolens_security_running: Scan running — findings will refresh automatically.
turbolens_security_permission_denied: You need the security_compliance permission to run or view scans.
turbolens_security_compliance_scan_descr: AI-assisted gap analysis against the selected regulations. Pick which regulations to include and run only when the metadata has actually changed.
turbolens_security_compliance_scan_start: Compliance scan started — progress will update below.
turbolens_security_scan_failed: Scan failed — click to see the error
turbolens_security_pick_regulation: Select at least one regulation before running a compliance scan.
turbolens_security_compliance_decision_h: Fresh from the scanner. Awaiting human review.
Out of scope for this finding.
